mechanismLead: Z-mount mirrorless uses same MakerNote tag 0x00A7 (ShutterCount) as other Nikon Z-series bodies already in cameras.json (Z6, Z7, Z9, etc.) — mechanism family confirmed and implemented.
source: Web search for Nikon Zfc specifications + exiftool tag reference
notes: APS-C mirrorless. Z-mount standard documented across all modern Nikon mirrorless. No sample file validation needed if treating as sibling to Z6/Z7/Z9 family already confirmed in engine.

mechanismLead: Consumer-level DSLR. Canon's documented policy: shutter count NOT stored in-file for consumer bodies, only readable via USB PTP. No tag 0x0093 entry found or documented.
source: Web search results confirm Canon USB-only policy for consumer CR2 DSLRs
notes: First Canon digital EOS. No in-file mechanism publicly documented.

mechanismLead: Consumer-level DSLR (10D line, 2003). Canon's documented policy: shutter count stored only via USB PTP for consumer bodies, NOT in CR2 file itself. Professional 1D/1Ds series (already in database) are the only Canon models with in-file shutter count via tag 0x0093.
source: Web search confirms Canon's strict USB-only policy for non-1D bodies
notes: Part of consumer xxD line. No in-file mechanism.

mechanismLead: Sony's first A-mount DSLR (2006). MakerNote tag 0x9050 confirmed to exist for Sony A-mount DSLRs (same tag as E-mount), but exact byte offset for this earliest generation is not publicly documented. Subsequent A-mount models (A700, A900) use same tag; offset varies by generation.
source: Web search confirms tag 0x9050 for Sony A-mount; exiftool Sony.pm reference indicates model-specific handling
notes: Earliest A-mount DSLR. Needs real sample file to reverse-engineer offset or verify against exiftool ground truth.

mechanismLead: Entry-level X-mount mirrorless (2012). All Fujifilm X-series models documented to use MakerNote tag 0x1438 (ImageCount), same tag already confirmed and implemented in engine for X-T1, X-T2, X-T3, etc. A1 is earliest X-A line but same mechanism family.
source: Web search confirms tag 0x1438 for Fujifilm X-series; Wikipedia lists X-A1 as 2012 X-mount body
notes: Entry-level X-mount. Tag 0x1438 already implemented; no sample needed if treating as X-series sibling.
